site.title: Furor — Dance Hyderabad
site.tagline: India's largest Latin dance school. Salsa, Bachata, West Coast Swing.
site.footerCopy: Furor Dance Hyderabad — Dance for life. Our home is in Jubilee Hills.

hero.headline: The night was made for *dancing*.
hero.subHeadline: Learn Salsa, Bachata and West Coast Swing with India's most loved Latin dance school. Our home is in Jubilee Hills, Hyderabad. Beginner-friendly. Whisper a yes — we'll take it from there on WhatsApp.

tonight.headline: La Rumba · Latin Social
tonight.body: Hyderabad's weekly Latin social. All levels welcome. Free for current students; ₹300 for guests.
tonight.ctaContext: the La Rumba Latin social tonight at Jubilee Hills

points.title: 16 years, 5 cities
points.body: Founded 2009. Today we're across Hyderabad, Bangalore, Pune, Ahmedabad and Gurgaon — but Hyderabad is home.

points.title: Built around the social
points.body: Class is one half. Our weekly social, La Rumba, is where the dance becomes yours.

points.title: True beginners welcome
points.body: Most of our students walked in having never danced. Our Foundation track is built for exactly that.

danceStyles.name: Salsa
danceStyles.tagline: The original Latin classic — fast, playful, infinitely connecting.
danceStyles.description: Salsa is the heartbeat of Latin dance — a partner dance born of Cuban son, mambo and Puerto Rican rhythms. We teach the New York and Cuban styles, building from the ground up so leading and following feel natural rather than memorised.
danceStyles.whoItsFor: If you've never partner-danced before and want one dance that opens every social floor in the world — this is it.

levelOutcomes.foundation: After 8 weeks you can lead or follow basic salsa for a full song with any partner.
levelOutcomes.intermediate: Cross-body lead variations, shines, and musicality. You start choosing what to dance, not just remembering steps.
levelOutcomes.advanced: Body movement, layering and full social fluency. You're the partner everyone wants to dance with.

faqs.a: No. We rotate partners in class, which is also the fastest way to learn.
Anything you can move in. Smooth-soled shoes if you have them — we don't sell anything.

faqs.q: How fit do I need to be?
faqs.a: Not at all. Our oldest student started at 64.

danceStyles.name: Bachata
danceStyles.tagline: Slower, closer, deeply musical. The dance that teaches you to listen.
danceStyles.description: Bachata comes from the Dominican Republic and is the most popular Latin partner dance in the world right now. We teach Sensual and Modern, with a foundation in the Dominican original. Slower than Salsa — and harder to forget.
danceStyles.whoItsFor: If you want a dance that feels like a conversation, not a performance — start here.

levelOutcomes.foundation: Basic step, side basic, simple turns and the iconic Bachata body roll. Enough to dance every song at the social.
levelOutcomes.intermediate: Modern combinations, Sensual lead/follow techniques, partner connection drills.
levelOutcomes.advanced: Musicality, dips, body waves, and the freedom to interpret a song as you hear it.

faqs.a: It can be — it can also be playful, joyful, even silly. The dance is what you bring to it.
Most of our Bachata students said the same in week one. Bachata is forgiving by design.

danceStyles.name: West Coast Swing
danceStyles.tagline: Smooth, modern, danced to anything. The improviser's partner dance.
danceStyles.description: West Coast Swing (WCS) is a modern partner dance born in California, known for its smooth, elastic feel and the ability to dance it to almost any song — pop, R&B, blues. It is the partner dance of musicality and improvisation.
danceStyles.whoItsFor: If you want a partner dance you can take to a wedding, a party or a jazz club and still feel right — WCS is it.

levelOutcomes.foundation: Anchor step, push break, sugar push, left-side pass, right-side pass — the whole alphabet of WCS.
levelOutcomes.intermediate: Variations, syncopations, leader/follower hijacks, and your own style begins to emerge.
levelOutcomes.advanced: Full musicality, body language conversation, competition-level connection.

faqs.q: Is WCS only at one of your branches?
faqs.a: Currently yes — Jubilee Hills. Chat with us on WhatsApp and we'll tell you the next batch.

instructors.name: Rishikesh Chhabra
instructors.shortBio:
Rish is first and foremost a Salsa promoter. He took up Ballroom dancing in college while reading Engineering at Assumption University, Thailand — studying under Ajarn Boonbunjong Uratanamanee and Ajarn Thanawat — and was introduced to the languages of the Rumba, Waltz, Tango, Foxtrot and other International Ballroom and Latin styles.

After relocating to Bangalore, a chance encounter with Salsa at a winter workshop at Alliance Francaise sparked a love affair that continues today. There he studied Rueda and Cuban Style under Cyrille Ngalle. Looking to learn LA Style, he found Alex Diaz teaching at a Bangalore club — and from there, there has been no looking back. He joined Furor as a dancer in 2008 and trained as an instructor.

He has taken workshops with Edie ‘Salsa Freak’, Eddie Torres, Dave Paris, Zoe Klein, Junior & Emily, Ricardo Murillo, Viviana Vargas, Juan Matos, Candy Mena, Alex Lee, Kimberly Nodora, and Terry & Cécile — each adding something unique to his journey.

After relocating to Hyderabad, Rish took on the responsibility of promoting Furor's unique blend of Salsa — laying the groundwork for its formal entry by jump-starting weekly Salsa Socials in the city. A technologist in a consulting firm by day, he brings experience in business, event management and IT to Furor, and manages its online presence alongside his role as Promoter. A poet, writer, musician and dancer, he balances his interests across the arts and sciences.

instructors.name: DJ Aditya
instructors.shortBio:
"If dancing salsa is my meditation, DJing salsa is my nirvana." This is the philosophy that drives DJ Aditya to be the best in the business. He discovered his love for Salsa quite by accident while studying in the USA — being there brought him into direct contact with the Latin population and he came to see their music as a reflection of their culture.

While having the privilege to dance in Indianapolis, Chicago and New York, he realised how varied Salsa music can be — and just how much of a challenge that poses while DJing. "Spinning at socials is a matter of feel. The challenge is getting the crowd to connect to the music. The dancers can be of varied levels and the song has to have something for all of them."

While DJing he impresses on the importance of smoothness in transition and moods in the music. "If asked what the greatest compliment can be to my DJing — I hope dancers feel that my style has flow. I like to think of it as taking the dancers on a journey, with each song beating a new path."

testimonials.text: Rish is a great teacher — patient, structured and able to explain things three different ways until one clicks. Six months in I'm dancing at every social.
What I love is the systematic approach. Each class builds on the last. And the instructors actually answer messages on WhatsApp at 11pm.
I came in to learn one routine for my sister's wedding. I left in love with dancing. Three years later I'm still here.
Tried two other studios in Hyderabad before Furor. The difference is the social culture — La Rumba is where you stop being a student and start being a dancer.

stories.title: 2nd Runner-up at Chennai Salsa Festival — Kalpana & Karthik
stories.excerpt: Kalpana and Karthik take home second runner-up at the Chennai Salsa Festival open challenge — Hyderabad represents.
stories.body: Our team rolled into Chennai for the Salsa Festival open challenge. Kalpana and Karthik took home second runner-up — a result of months of practice, late-night socials and a partnership that just works. Congratulations to both of them, and to everyone who showed up to cheer.

stories.title: Taste of Salsa — Free Demo & Workshop
stories.excerpt: A free demo for anyone curious about Salsa. No partner needed. No prior dance experience required.
stories.body: Wondering if Salsa is for you? Come to a free Taste of Salsa demo. We'll teach you the basic step, the partner change, and let you dance to one real Salsa song before you decide anything. No partner needed — we rotate. No experience required. Message us on WhatsApp to grab a spot.

stories.title: La Rumba Christmas Ball — 24th December 2022
stories.excerpt: An elegant Christmas Ball. Daytime workshops, evening red-carpet social. The biggest La Rumba of the year.
stories.body: Once a year La Rumba dresses up. The Christmas Ball is daytime workshops followed by an evening red-carpet social. Dress code is elegant. The room is full of dancers from every level. If you've ever wanted to see what your year of training looks like on a real night out — this is it.

stories.title: Social Dancing Etiquette
stories.excerpt: A short guide to surviving — and shining at — your first social. Bright clothes, confidence, and a few things nobody tells you.
stories.body: Your first social is intimidating. Here's what helps: dress brightly so leaders find you, accept dances even when you're nervous, thank your partner at the end, and don't apologise mid-dance — finish the song. The room is much friendlier than it looks. Promise.

stories.title: La Rumba Latin Pool Party — 23rd November 2019
stories.excerpt: Workshops, poolside dancing and a sunset social at Marriott Executive Apartments Gachibowli.
stories.body: Workshops in the afternoon, poolside Salsa and Bachata as the sun went down, and a full social into the night. Marriott Executive Apartments Gachibowli hosted us, and the room was packed with dancers from across the city.

stories.title: Valentine Salsa Workshop — 14th February 2021
stories.excerpt: Two hours of partner dancing as a Valentine's gift — to yourself, or to someone else.
stories.body: A two-hour introduction to partner Salsa, designed for couples and friends who want a different kind of Valentine's evening. No experience needed. Bring a partner or come alone — we rotate.

stories.title: Wow!
stories.excerpt: Wow Magazine covers our Saturday Salsa socials — and the people who make them what they are.
stories.body: Wow Magazine ran a feature on our Saturday Salsa socials. The photographs are by their team and they capture something we struggle to put into words: the moment a beginner stops counting steps and just starts dancing.

stories.title: West Coast Swing Christmas Bootcamp — 24–25 December
stories.excerpt: A 12-hour weekend bootcamp covering the full WCS beginner syllabus. Open level.
stories.body: Twelve hours over two days. Push break, sugar push, side passes, anchor step — the full WCS beginner alphabet, plus enough musicality work that you'll be able to dance every song at our weekly socials by Sunday night. Open level: complete beginners welcome.

howItWorks.headline: Three steps from curious to dancing.

steps.title: Pick a style
steps.body: Beginner Latin covers Salsa or Bachata. Tell us what catches your ear — morning or evening batches both run.

steps.title: Register on WhatsApp
steps.body: Block your seat with a small token. We send dates, the studio address and what to bring — all on WhatsApp.

steps.title: Show up and dance
steps.body: Classes plus the weekly social. Miss one? Use our flexible make-up policy — no extra cost.

closingCta.headline: Ready when *you* are. We're one tap away.
closingCta.body: Tell us which style sounds like you. We'll handle the rest in WhatsApp — class times, prices, what to bring.

intro.headline: Sixteen years. Five cities. One love letter to dance.

introParagraphs: Furor began in 2009 in Bangalore with a single Salsa class. Today we're India's largest Latin dance school — across Bangalore, Hyderabad, Pune, Ahmedabad and Gurgaon — but we've never lost the thing that started it: a room, a song, two people learning to listen.
In Hyderabad our home is the Jubilee Hills studio, and our weekly Latin social — La Rumba — is where students stop being students and start being dancers.

timeline.headline: From one Salsa class in Bangalore to five cities.

milestones.title: Furor begins in Bangalore
milestones.body: Founded by Creative Director Alex Diaz with one mission: transform India's Salsa scene.

milestones.title: Hyderabad opens
milestones.body: Rishikesh Chhabra brings Furor to Hyderabad — regular classes and Saturday socials from day one.

milestones.title: Ahmedabad joins in
milestones.body: Jayvir Mehta starts Furor Ahmedabad, building the Latin scene from scratch.

milestones.title: Pune officially launches
milestones.body: After three years as enMotion Pune, the city joins the Furor family under Yatin Ranade and Supreme Kaur Bhamra.

milestones.title: Online classes go live
milestones.body: The pandemic pushes us online — hybrid teaching becomes part of how we work.

milestones.title: Gurgaon — city number five
milestones.body: Furor opens its fifth city under Pummy Kumari, cementing its place as India's largest Latin dance academy.

closingCta.headline: Want to come dance?
closingCta.body: We're one tap away. WhatsApp is fastest.

intro.headline: The questions we hear most.
intro.lead: Anything missing? WhatsApp us and we'll answer in minutes.

items.q: I have never danced before. Can I still join?
items.a: Yes. Most of our students walk in having never partner-danced before. Our Foundation track is built for absolute beginners — we teach you the basic step, the connection, and the social etiquette from scratch.

items.q: Do I need a partner to join?
items.a: No. We rotate partners in class — it is the fastest way to learn and also how social dancing works. You will dance with leaders and followers of every level.

items.q: What should I wear to class?
items.a: Anything comfortable that lets you move. Smooth-soled shoes help (you turn easier) but they are not required for your first class. Avoid sticky rubber soles if you can.

items.q: How fit do I need to be?
items.a: You do not need to be particularly fit. We teach dancers from age 16 to 70+. Our oldest beginner started at 64.
Foundation batches run for 2 months — typically 20 hours total, with weekend or weekday sessions. Intermediate and Advanced courses follow the same rhythm.
Foundation Salsa and Bachata are ₹6,500 for the full 2-month batch. Intermediate courses are ₹7,500. Pricing is per person, not per couple.

items.q: What if I miss a class?
items.a: We have a flexible make-up policy — message us on WhatsApp and we will find you a session in another batch of the same level.
All Furor Hyderabad batches are at our Jubilee Hills studio — 2nd Floor, Alcazar Mall, Road No. 36, Jubilee Hills, Hyderabad 500033.

items.q: How long has Furor been around?
items.a: Furor was founded in 2009 in Bangalore by Alex Diaz, and opened in Hyderabad in 2010 under Rishikesh Chhabra. Today we are India's largest Latin dance school across Bangalore, Hyderabad, Pune, Ahmedabad and Gurgaon.

La Rumba is our weekly Latin social — Hyderabad's longest-running Salsa and Bachata night. Free for current students, ₹300 for guests. It is where students stop being students and start being dancers.

items.q: Do you do corporate workshops or events?
items.a: Yes. We design dance experiences for corporates, product launches and private events — one-hour workshops up to multi-week programs. WhatsApp us for a custom quote.
WhatsApp is fastest: +91 88860 72572. You can also email furorhyd@dancehyderabad.com or DM @furorhyd on Instagram.

closingCta.headline: Still have questions?
closingCta.body: WhatsApp is the fastest way to reach us — we answer in minutes.

intro.headline: One tap away. We're listening.
intro.lead: WhatsApp is fastest. Email if you'd rather. Walk in if you're in the neighbourhood.

tiles.whatsappBody: Reply in minutes during studio hours. Slower late nights, but we get to it.
tiles.emailBody: Best for corporate enquiries, partnerships and longer questions that need attachments.
tiles.instagramBody: DM us on Instagram — see the dance floor before you book a class.

closingCta.headline: Ready when you are.
closingCta.body: Tell us which style sounds like you. We'll handle the rest in WhatsApp — class times, prices, what to bring.
